Title: GhBZR3 suppresses cotton fiber elongation by inhibiting very-long-chain fatty acid biosynthesis

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Very-long-chain fatty acids (especially C24 and C26 fatty acids)
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: fatty acid elongation from C20 to C26 VLCFAs
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: committed step / elongation-condensation step in VLCFA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: 3-ketoacyl-CoA elongated intermediates leading to C20-C26 VLCFAs
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies precursor pool and directly drives synthesis of target VLCFAs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: specifically and highly expressed in cotton fibers
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: specifically and highly expressed in fibers; reduced in GhKCS13-VIGS plants; repressed by GhBZR3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing in cotton; RT-qPCR; fiber length phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native GhKCS13 promoter analyzed in Y1H, EMSA and luciferase reporter assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhKCS13 is specifically and highly expressed in cotton fibers. 2. Silencing GhKCS13 significantly shortened mature fiber length without affecting fiber initiation. 3. GhKCS13 acts positively in fiber cell elongation and is implicated in VLCFA synthesis. 4. GhKCS13 is a direct downstream target repressed by GhBZR3.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes VLCFA accumulation and cotton fiber elongation; silencing reduces fiber length
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising target to increase cotton fiber length by enhancing VLCFA biosynthesis, potentially through activation/editing of GhKCS13 or release from GhBZR3 repression
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper cites prior evidence that GhKCS13 participates in synthesis of C20-C26 VLCFAs. In this study, its metabolic role is supported mainly by expression, regulatory binding by GhBZR3, and VIGS phenotyping rather than direct enzyme assay.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Very-long-chain fatty acids (especially C24 and C26 fatty acids)
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BZR1 family transcription factor; atypical BES1/BZR1 family protein with DNA-binding domain, C-terminal domain, and nuclear localization signal; lacks canonical functional 14-3-3 binding motif behavior
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: mainly expressed in pistil, stem and leaf; expression induced by cold (6 h), heat (6 h), and PEG (12 h); lower than other GhBZR genes during fiber development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: mainly expressed in pistil, stem and leaf; lower during fiber developmental process; silenced in VIGS plants; overexpressed in cotton and Arabidopsis transgenics
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: brassinosteroid signaling; BRZ treatment used to probe downstream pathways; abiotic stress including cold, heat and PEG induces expression
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: VLCFA biosynthesis during cotton fiber elongation
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: directly suppresses GhKCS13 expression; GhKCS6 and GhKCS12 are upregulated after GhBZR3 silencing but not shown as direct targets
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: BZR family generally acts in BR feedback regulation, but specific GhBZR3 feedback on metabolite pathway not established
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: negative regulator of cotton fiber VLCFA accumulation and fiber elongation through direct repression of a key VLCFA biosynthetic gene
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS silencing in cotton; cotton overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; Arabidopsis overexpression; RNA-seq; GC-MS fatty acid profiling; Y1H; EMSA; luciferase assay; RT-qPCR; subcellular localization
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter in Arabidopsis overexpression; 35S promoter in cotton overexpression constructs
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhBZR3 is an atypical BZR transcription factor localized mainly in the nucleus and capable of transcriptional activity. 2. Silencing or knockout of GhBZR3 increased plant height, fiber length, and VLCFA accumulation, especially C24 and C26 species. 3. Overexpression of GhBZR3 reduced cotton fiber cell length and inhibited hypocotyl, root, and root hair elongation in Arabidopsis. 4. GhBZR3 directly binds the GhKCS13 promoter and represses its expression, thereby lowering VLCFA biosynthesis and suppressing fiber elongation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduces VLCFA accumulation and suppresses cotton fiber elongation; loss of function increases VLCFAs and fiber length
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: BRZ treatment of ovules for 6 h or 12 h; abiotic stress treatments including cold, heat, and PEG
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression inhibits general cell elongation, causing shorter fiber cells, hypocotyls, roots, and root hairs; silencing alters plant architecture with taller plants and smaller branch angles
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockout, silencing, or weakening promoter activity of GhBZR3 could be used to increase VLCFA content and improve cotton fiber length, but broader effects on plant architecture and growth should be monitored
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GhBZR3 is most homologous to AtBEH4. Its 14-3-3 binding motif is mutated and loss of this motif did not alter transcriptional activity, indicating a distinct regulatory mechanism from canonical BZR1/BES1 proteins.
